21	@dataclasses.dataclass
22	class SamplingBatchInfo:
23	    # Basic batched sampling params
24	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
25	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
26	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
27	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
28	
29	    # Whether all requests use greedy sampling
30	    is_all_greedy: bool
31	
32	    # Whether any requests use top_p sampling
33	    need_top_p_sampling: bool
34	
35	    # Whether any requests use top_k sampling
36	    need_top_k_sampling: bool
37	
38	    # Whether any request needs min_p sampling
39	    need_min_p_sampling: bool
40	
41	    # Masking tensors for grammar-guided structured outputs
42	    vocab_size: int
43	    grammars: Optional[List] = None
44	    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
45	    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
46	
47	    # Penalizer
48	    penalizer_orchestrator: Optional[penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator] = None
49	    acc_linear_penalties: torch.Tensor = None  # Used in the overlap mode
50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor
52	    has_custom_logit_processor: bool = False
53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
68	
69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret
185	
186	    # placeholder for override
187	    def adjusted_from_schedule_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
188	        pass
189	
190	    # placeholder for override
191	    def adjusted_merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
192	        pass
193	
194	    def __len__(self):
195	        return len(self.temperatures)
196	
197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None
201	            return
202	
203	        # Find a grammar from the list
204	        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
205	
206	        # TODO(lianmin): Maybe we can reuse the existing mask?
207	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
208	            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
209	            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
210	            device=self.device,
211	        )
212	        self.apply_mask_func = (
213	            first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
214	        )  # force to use static method
215	
216	        # Apply the mask
217	        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
218	            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
219	                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
220	
221	        # Move the mask to the device if needed
222	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
223	
224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),
228	                dtype=torch.float32,
229	                device=self.temperatures.device,
230	            )
231	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(self.acc_linear_penalties)
232	        else:
233	            self.acc_linear_penalties = None
234	
235	    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
236	        if self.acc_linear_penalties is not None:
237	            # Used in the overlap mode
238	            logits.add_(self.acc_linear_penalties)
239	
240	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator and self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode
242	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(logits)
243	
244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
245	            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
246	
247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
248	            logits.add_(self.logit_bias)
249	
250	    def filter_batch(self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor):
251	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.filter(keep_indices_device)
252	
253	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor:
254	            self._filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(keep_indices, keep_indices_device)
255	
256	        for item in [
257	            "temperatures",
258	            "top_ps",
259	            "top_ks",
260	            "min_ps",
261	            "sampling_seed",
262	        ]:
263	            value = getattr(self, item, None)
264	            if value is not None:
265	                setattr(self, item, value[keep_indices_device])
266	
267	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
268	            self.logit_bias = self.logit_bias[keep_indices_device]
269	
270	    def _filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(
271	        self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor
272	    ):
273	        """Filter the custom logit processor and custom params"""
274	        self.custom_logit_processor = {
275	            k: (p, mask[keep_indices_device])
276	            for k, (p, mask) in self.custom_logit_processor.items()
277	            if torch.any(
278	                mask[keep_indices_device]
279	            )  # ignore the custom logit processor whose mask is all False
280	        }
281	        self.custom_params = [self.custom_params[i] for i in keep_indices]
282	
283	        # If the custom logit processor is an empty dict, set the flag to False,
284	        # and set the custom logit processor and custom params to None.
285	        if len(self.custom_logit_processor) == 0:
286	            self.custom_logit_processor = None
287	            self.custom_params = None
288	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = False
289	
290	    @staticmethod
291	    def merge_custom_logit_processor(
292	        lhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
293	        rhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
294	        bs1: int,
295	        bs2: int,
296	        device: str,
297	    ):
298	        if lhs is None and rhs is None:
299	            return None
300	        lhs, rhs = lhs or {}, rhs or {}
301	
302	        keys = set(lhs.keys()).union(set(rhs.keys()))
303	        merged_dict = {}
304	
305	        for k in keys:
306	            # Get the logit processor object
307	            processor = lhs[k][0] if k in lhs else rhs[k][0]
308	            # Get and merge the mask tensors from the two dicts
309	            left_mask = (
310	                lhs[k][1]
311	                if k in lhs
312	                else torch.zeros(bs1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
313	            )
314	            right_mask = (
315	                rhs[k][1]
316	                if k in rhs
317	                else torch.zeros(bs2, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
318	            )
319	            merged_dict[k] = (processor, torch.cat([left_mask, right_mask]))
320	
321	            assert merged_dict[k][1].shape[0] == bs1 + bs2, (
322	                f"The batch size of merged mask ({merged_dict[k][1].shape[0]}) does not match "
323	                f"the sum of the batch sizes of the two masks ({bs1 + bs2})"
324	                f"\n{left_mask=}\n{right_mask=}\n{bs1=}\n{bs2=}"
325	                f"\n{lhs=}\n{rhs=}"
326	            )
327	
328	        return merged_dict
329	
330	    def merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
331	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.merge(other.penalizer_orchestrator)
332	
333	        # Merge the custom logit processors and custom params lists
334	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor or other.has_custom_logit_processor:
335	            # Merge the custom logit processors
336	            self.custom_logit_processor = (
337	                SamplingBatchInfo.merge_custom_logit_processor(
338	                    self.custom_logit_processor,
339	                    other.custom_logit_processor,
340	                    len(self),
341	                    len(other),
342	                    self.device,
343	                )
344	            )
345	            # Merge the custom params lists
346	            self.custom_params = self.custom_params or [None] * len(self)
347	            other.custom_params = other.custom_params or [None] * len(other)
348	            self.custom_params.extend(other.custom_params)
349	
350	            # Set the flag to True if any of the two has custom logit processor
351	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = True
352	
353	        # Merge logit bias - note this has to come before the temperatures tensor update! Otherwise will cause crashes.
354	        # See note below on len(self) and len(other).
355	        self.logit_bias = merge_bias_tensor(
356	            self.logit_bias, other.logit_bias, len(self), len(other), self.device, 0.0
357	        )
358	
359	        # Note: because the __len()__ operator is defined on the temperatures tensor,
360	        # please make sure any merge operation with len(self) or len(other) is done before
361	        # the merge operation of the temperatures tensor below.
362	        for item in [
363	            "temperatures",
364	            "top_ps",
365	            "top_ks",
366	            "min_ps",
367	            "sampling_seed",
368	        ]:
369	            self_val = getattr(self, item, None)
370	            other_val = getattr(other, item, None)
371	            if self_val is not None and other_val is not None:
372	                setattr(self, item, torch.cat([self_val, other_val]))
373	
374	        self.is_all_greedy &= other.is_all_greedy
375	        self.need_top_p_sampling |= other.need_top_p_sampling
376	        self.need_top_k_sampling |= other.need_top_k_sampling
377	        self.need_min_p_sampling |= other.need_min_p_sampling
378	
379	        self.adjusted_merge_batch(other)
380	
381	    def copy_for_forward(self):
382	        # Accumulate the penalty into a pre-allocated buffer to get rid of the dependency of `penalizer_orchestrator` later
383	        self.update_penalties()
384	        return dataclasses.replace(self, penalizer_orchestrator=None)
385	
386	
387	def merge_bias_tensor(
388	    lhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
389	    rhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
390	    bs1: int,
391	    bs2: int,
392	    device: str,
393	    default: float,
394	):
395	    """Merge two bias tensors for batch merging.
396	
397	    Args:
398	        lhs: Left-hand side tensor
399	        rhs: Right-hand side tensor
400	        bs1: Batch size of left-hand side tensor
401	        bs2: Batch size of right-hand side tensor
402	        device: Device to place the merged tensor on
403	        default: Default value for missing tensor elements
404	
405	    Returns:
406	        Merged tensor or None if both inputs are None
407	    """
408	    if lhs is None and rhs is None:
409	        return None
410	
411	    if lhs is not None and rhs is not None:
412	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
413	    else:
414	        if lhs is not None:
415	            shape, dtype = lhs.shape[1:], lhs.dtype
416	        else:
417	            shape, dtype = rhs.shape[1:], rhs.dtype
418	
419	        if lhs is None:
420	            lhs = torch.empty((bs1, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
421	        if rhs is None:
422	            rhs = torch.empty((bs2, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
423	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
424

17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,
57	            self.return_logprob,
58	            self.logprob_start_len,
59	            self.top_logprobs_num,
60	            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
61	            self.json_schema,
62	        )
63	
64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }
78	
79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }
92	
93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
103	            ),
104	            "temperature": self.temperature,
105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }
108	
109	    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
110	        if self.regex is not None:
111	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
112	        return {
113	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
114	            "stop": self.stop or None,
115	            "temperature": self.temperature,
116	            "top_p": self.top_p,
117	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
118	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
119	        }
120	
121	    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
122	        return {
123	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
124	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
125	            "n": self.n,
126	            "stop": self.stop,
127	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
128	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
129	            "temperature": self.temperature,
130	            "top_p": self.top_p,
131	            "top_k": self.top_k,
132	            "min_p": self.min_p,
133	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
134	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
135	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
136	            "regex": self.regex,
137	            "json_schema": self.json_schema,
138	        }

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'

17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

18	import torch

21	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,

27	class ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
28	    def __init__(self, grammar: BaseGrammarObject, think_end_id: int):
29	        super().__init__()
30	        self.grammar = grammar
31	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
32	        # -1    means thinking has not ended yet
33	        # 0     means just ended thinking in the last token
34	        # +     means number of tokens after thinking ended
35	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
36	
37	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
38	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1 if reasoning else 0
39	
40	    def transfer_state(self, token: int) -> int:
41	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == -1 and token == self.think_end_id:
42	            self.tokens_after_think_end = 0
43	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
44	            self.tokens_after_think_end += 1
45	
46	    def rollback_state(self):
47	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == 0:
48	            self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
49	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end > 0:
50	            self.tokens_after_think_end -= 1
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
53	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
54	            self.grammar.accept_token(token)
55	        self.transfer_state(token)
56	
57	    def is_terminated(self):
58	        return self.grammar.is_terminated()
59	
60	    def rollback(self, k):
61	        steps_after_think = min(k, self.tokens_after_think_end)
62	        if steps_after_think > 0:
63	            self.grammar.rollback(steps_after_think)
64	
65	        for _ in range(k):
66	            self.rollback_state()
67	
68	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
69	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
70	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
71	        return self.grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
72	
73	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
74	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
75	            self.grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)
76	
77	    def move_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
78	        return self.grammar.move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)
79	
80	    @property
81	    def apply_vocab_mask(self):
82	        return self.grammar.apply_vocab_mask
83	
84	    def copy(self) -> BaseGrammarObject:
85	        return ReasonerGrammarObject(self.grammar.copy(), self.think_end_id)
86	
87	    @property
88	    def finished(self):
89	        return self.grammar.finished
90	
91	    @finished.setter
92	    def finished(self, finished):
93	        self.grammar.finished = finished
94	
95	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer):
96	        return self.grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)
97	
98	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper):
99	        return self.grammar.jump_forward_str_state(helper)
100	
101	    def jump_and_retokenize(
102	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
103	    ):
104	        return self.grammar.jump_and_retokenize(
105	            old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state
106	        )
107	
108	
109	class ReasonerGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	    def __init__(self, grammar_backend: BaseGrammarBackend, think_end_id):
111	        super().__init__()
112	        self.grammar_backend = grammar_backend
113	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
114	
115	    def _init_value_dispatch(
116	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], reasoning: bool
117	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
118	        ret = self.grammar_backend._init_value_dispatch(key, reasoning)
119	        # avoid wrapping invalid grammar, so that the scheduler can detect it
120	        if ret is None or ret is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
121	            return ret
122	        obj = ReasonerGrammarObject(ret, self.think_end_id)
123	        obj.maybe_init_reasoning(reasoning)
124	        return obj

21	@dataclasses.dataclass
22	class SamplingBatchInfo:
23	    # Basic batched sampling params
24	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
25	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
26	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
27	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
28	
29	    # Whether all requests use greedy sampling
30	    is_all_greedy: bool
31	
32	    # Whether any requests use top_p sampling
33	    need_top_p_sampling: bool
34	
35	    # Whether any requests use top_k sampling
36	    need_top_k_sampling: bool
37	
38	    # Whether any request needs min_p sampling
39	    need_min_p_sampling: bool
40	
41	    # Masking tensors for grammar-guided structured outputs
42	    vocab_size: int
43	    grammars: Optional[List] = None
44	    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
45	    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
46	
47	    # Penalizer
48	    penalizer_orchestrator: Optional[penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator] = None
49	    acc_linear_penalties: torch.Tensor = None  # Used in the overlap mode
50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor
52	    has_custom_logit_processor: bool = False
53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
68	
69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret
185	
186	    # placeholder for override
187	    def adjusted_from_schedule_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
188	        pass
189	
190	    # placeholder for override
191	    def adjusted_merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
192	        pass
193	
194	    def __len__(self):
195	        return len(self.temperatures)
196	
197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None
201	            return
202	
203	        # Find a grammar from the list
204	        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
205	
206	        # TODO(lianmin): Maybe we can reuse the existing mask?
207	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
208	            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
209	            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
210	            device=self.device,
211	        )
212	        self.apply_mask_func = (
213	            first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
214	        )  # force to use static method
215	
216	        # Apply the mask
217	        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
218	            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
219	                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
220	
221	        # Move the mask to the device if needed
222	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
223	
224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),
228	                dtype=torch.float32,
229	                device=self.temperatures.device,
230	            )
231	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(self.acc_linear_penalties)
232	        else:
233	            self.acc_linear_penalties = None
234	
235	    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
236	        if self.acc_linear_penalties is not None:
237	            # Used in the overlap mode
238	            logits.add_(self.acc_linear_penalties)
239	
240	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator and self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode
242	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(logits)
243	
244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
245	            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
246	
247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
248	            logits.add_(self.logit_bias)
249	
250	    def filter_batch(self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor):

23	def function(

25	):
26	    if func:
27	        return SglFunction(func, num_api_spec_tokens=num_api_spec_tokens)
28	
29	    def decorator(func):
30	        return SglFunction(func, num_api_spec_tokens=num_api_spec_tokens)
31	
32	    return decorator

36	    # Avoid importing unnecessary dependency

53	def flush_cache(backend: Optional[BaseBackend] = None):
54	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
55	    if backend is None:
56	        return False
57	
58	    # If backend is Runtime
59	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
60	        backend = backend.endpoint
61	    return backend.flush_cache()

64	def get_server_info(backend: Optional[BaseBackend] = None):
65	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
66	    if backend is None:
67	        return None
68	
69	    # If backend is Runtime
70	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
71	        backend = backend.endpoint
72	    return backend.get_server_info()

75	def gen(
76	    name: Optional[str] = None,
77	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
78	    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
79	    n: Optional[int] = None,
80	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
81	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
82	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
83	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
84	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
85	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
86	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
87	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
88	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
89	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
90	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
91	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
92	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
93	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
94	    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
95	    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
96	    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
97	    regex: Optional[str] = None,
98	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
99	):
100	    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
101	
102	    if choices:
103	        return SglSelect(
104	            name,
105	            choices,
106	            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
107	            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
108	        )
109	
110	    # check regex is valid
111	    if regex is not None:
112	        try:
113	            re.compile(regex)
114	        except re.error as e:
115	            raise e
116	
117	    return SglGen(
118	        name,
119	        max_tokens,
120	        min_tokens,
121	        n,
122	        stop,
123	        stop_token_ids,
124	        stop_regex,
125	        temperature,
126	        top_p,
127	        top_k,
128	        min_p,
129	        frequency_penalty,
130	        presence_penalty,
131	        ignore_eos,
132	        return_logprob,
133	        logprob_start_len,
134	        top_logprobs_num,
135	        return_text_in_logprobs,
136	        dtype,
137	        regex,
138	        json_schema,
139	    )

61	def _extract_max_dynamic_patch(request: ChatCompletionRequest):
62	    img_vals = []
63	    vid_vals = []
64	    for msg in request.messages or []:
65	        content = getattr(msg, "content", None)
66	        if not isinstance(content, list):
67	            continue
68	        for part in content:
69	            # pydantic object or dict type
70	            if getattr(part, "type", None) == "image_url":
71	                iu = getattr(part, "image_url", None)
72	                mdp = getattr(iu, "max_dynamic_patch", None) if iu else None
73	                if mdp is not None:
74	                    img_vals.append(int(mdp))
75	            elif getattr(part, "type", None) == "video_url":
76	                vu = getattr(part, "video_url", None)
77	                mdp = getattr(vu, "max_dynamic_patch", None) if vu else None
78	                if mdp is not None:
79	                    vid_vals.append(int(mdp))
80	
81	    # TODO(yuan-luo): per-item max_dynamic_patch for both image and video
82	    img_max_dynamic_patch = min(img_vals) if img_vals else None
83	    vid_max_dynamic_patch = min(vid_vals) if vid_vals else None
84	    return img_max_dynamic_patch, vid_max_dynamic_patch

124	    def _handle_last_assistant_message(
125	        self,
126	        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],
127	        request: ChatCompletionRequest,
128	    ) -> tuple[List[Dict[str, Any]], Optional[str]]:
129	        """
130	        Handle continue_final_message feature: separate final assistant message.
131	
132	        If continue_final_message is enabled and the last message is from assistant,
133	        extract its content and remove it from the message list.
134	        If continue_final_message is False and the last message is from assistant,
135	        convert it to a user message to ensure the last message is always from user.
136	
137	        Only processes text-based content (strings), ignoring multimodal content (lists).
138	
139	        Args:
140	            messages: List of message dictionaries
141	            request: ChatCompletionRequest with continue_final_message flag
142	
143	        Returns:
144	            Tuple of (processed_messages, assistant_prefix)
145	            - processed_messages: Messages with last assistant message handled appropriately
146	            - assistant_prefix: Content of the last assistant message (string only), or None
147	        """
148	        assistant_prefix = None
149	        if messages and messages[-1].get("role") == "assistant":
150	            last_content = messages[-1].get("content")
151	            # Only process string content, ignore multimodal content (lists)
152	            if isinstance(last_content, str):
153	                if request.continue_final_message:
154	                    # Extract content and remove the assistant message
155	                    assistant_prefix = last_content
156	                    messages = messages[:-1]
157	                else:
158	                    # Convert the last assistant message to user message
159	                    messages[-1] = {"role": "user", "content": last_content}
160	        return messages, assistant_prefix

162	    def _append_assistant_prefix_to_prompt_ids(
163	        self, prompt_ids: List[int], assistant_prefix: str
164	    ) -> List[int]:
165	        """
166	        Append assistant prefix to prompt_ids.
167	
168	        Args:
169	            prompt_ids: Current prompt token IDs
170	            assistant_prefix: Assistant message content to append
171	
172	        Returns:
173	            Updated prompt_ids with assistant prefix appended
174	        """
175	        encoded = self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer.encode(assistant_prefix)
176	        if encoded and encoded[0] == self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer.bos_token_id:
177	            encoded = encoded[1:]
178	        return prompt_ids + encoded

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None):
        
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
            key, req.require_reasoning
        )
```

### 4. **Three Grammar Backend Implementations**

#### **A. XGrammar Backend**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`

```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    try:
        if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
        else:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
                schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
            )
    except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
```

- Uses `xgrammar.GrammarCompiler.compile_json_schema()` to convert JSON schema to compiled grammar
- Handles special case `$$ANY$$` for any valid JSON

#### **B. Outlines Backend**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`


#### **C. LLGuidance Backend**

```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
    try:
        serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
            key_string,
            defaults={
                "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
                "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
            },
        )
    except Exception as e:

        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
```

- Uses `llguidance.LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema()` to build grammar

### 6. **Tool/Function Call JSON Schema Support**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py`

Complete JSON schema generation for tool calling (lines 207-246):
```python
def get_json_schema_constraint(
    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
) -> Optional[dict]:
    """Get the JSON schema constraint for the specified tool choice."""
    
    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
        for tool in tools:
            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
                return {
                    "type": "array",
                    "minItems": 1,
                    "maxItems": 1,
                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
                }
        return None
    elif tool_choice == "required":
        json_schema = {
            "type": "array",
            "minItems": 1,
            "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
            },
        }
        json_schema_defs = _get_tool_schema_defs(tools)
        if json_schema_defs:
            json_schema["$defs"] = json_schema_defs
        return json_schema
    return None
```

Also includes utility functions:
- `infer_type_from_json_schema()` (lines 104-204) - Infers Python type from JSON schema
- `_get_tool_schema_defs()` (lines 60-87) - Consolidates `$defs` from multiple tools with conflict detection
- `_partial_json_loads()` (lines 23-49) - Parses incomplete JSON during streaming using `partial_json_parser`

---

### 7. **JSON Validation Utilities**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/python/sglang/utils.py`

```python
# Lines 53-80
def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
    """Convert a JSON schema to a string.
    
    Supports:
    - dict: JSON serialize
    - str: pass through
    - Pydantic BaseModel subclass: extract model_json_schema()
    """
    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema)
    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
        schema_str = json_schema
    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
    else:
        raise ValueError(...)
    return schema_str
```

### 8. **Test Files for JSON Constraints**

**A. JSON Schema Tool Constraint Tests**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py`

**C. JSON Constrained Generation Kit**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py`

- Tests JSON schema constraints via `/generate` endpoint
- Tests OpenAI API `response_format` with `json_schema` type
- Validates output against schema using `jsonschema.validate()`

---

### 9. **Benchmark Files**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/benchmark/json_schema/bench_sglang.py`

```python
# Lines 20-26
@sgl.function
def schema_gen(s, message: Tuple[str, str], json_schema: str):
    system, user = message
    s += sgl.system(system)
    s += sgl.user(user)
    s += sgl.assistant(
        sgl.gen("json_output", temperature=0, max_tokens=256, json_schema=json_schema)
    )
```

Uses dataset: `NousResearch/json-mode-eval`

---

### 10. **Partial JSON Parser Integration**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` (lines 1-58)

Uses `partial_json_parser` library with `Allow` flags:
- `Allow.STR` - Allow partial strings
- `Allow.OBJ` - Allow partial objects
- `Allow.ARR` - Allow partial arrays
- `Allow.ALL` - Allow all types

This is used for streaming JSON output where the response is incomplete.

---

### Key Findings:

1. **No JSONPath Libraries**: The project doesn't use jsonpath-ng, jsonpath, or jmespath. Instead, it focuses on full JSON schema validation and grammar-constrained generation.

2. **Three Constraint Backends**: XGrammar (fastest, most feature-rich), Outlines (via regex conversion), and LLGuidance (native support).

3. **Multiple Constraint Types**: Supports `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, and `structural_tag` (unified constraint model).

4. **Streaming JSON Support**: Uses `partial_json_parser` for handling incomplete JSON during streaming generation.

5. **Tool Calling**: Full JSON schema generation for OpenAI-compatible tool calling with `$defs` consolidation and conflict detection.

6. **API Integration**: Full OpenAI-compatible API with `response_format` supporting both `json_object` and `json_schema` types.

```python
def get_json_schema_constraint(
    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
) -> Optional[dict]:
    """Get the JSON schema constraint for the specified tool choice."""
    
    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
        for tool in tools:
            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
                return {
                    "type": "array",
                    "minItems": 1,
                    "maxItems": 1,
                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
                }
        return None
    elif tool_choice == "required":
        json_schema = {
            "type": "array",
            "minItems": 1,
            "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
            },
        }
        json_schema_defs = _get_tool_schema_defs(tools)
        if json_schema_defs:
            json_schema["$defs"] = json_schema_defs
        return json_schema
    return None
```

```python
def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
    """Convert a JSON schema to a string.
    
    Supports:
    - dict: JSON serialize
    - str: pass through
    - Pydantic BaseModel subclass: extract model_json_schema()
    """
    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema)
    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
        schema_str = json_schema
    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
    else:
        raise ValueError(...)
    return schema_str
```

## Output Constraints Architecture in SGLang

### 1. Overview

SGLang implements output constraints through a modular grammar-based system that supports multiple constraint types: **regex**, **JSON schema**, **EBNF**, and **structural tags**. The system uses backend implementations (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance) to enforce these constraints during token generation.

---

### 2. Key Files and Their Locations

   - Defines `SglSamplingParams` dataclass with constraint fields: `json_schema`, `regex`, and `dtype`
   - Defines `SglGen` class that wraps sampling parameters including constraints
   - Contains regex patterns for basic types (REGEX_INT, REGEX_FLOAT, REGEX_BOOL, REGEX_STR)

2. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/python/sglang/lang/api.py`**
   - Public API function `gen()` accepts `regex` and `json_schema` parameters
   - Validates regex syntax before creating SglGen object

3. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`**
   - `SamplingParams` class with constraint fields: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
   - Validates that only ONE of these constraints is set at a time (mutually exclusive)

#### **Grammar Backend Architecture Files:**

4. **`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`**

   - `BaseGrammarObject`: Abstract base for all grammar implementations

### 3. Class Hierarchy for Constraints

```
BaseGrammarObject (abstract base)
├── XGrammarGrammar (xgrammar backend)
├── OutlinesGrammar (outlines backend)
├── GuidanceGrammar (llguidance backend)
└── ReasonerGrammarObject (wrapper for reasoning mode)

BaseGrammarBackend (abstract dispatcher)
├── XGrammarGrammarBackend
├── OutlinesGrammarBackend
├── GuidanceBackend
└── ReasonerGrammarBackend (wrapper)

GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY (global registry for custom backends)
```

---

### 4. Constraint Registration and Dispatch

#### **Registration:**
- Backends are created in `create_grammar_backend()` in `base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 206-274)
- Custom backends can be registered via `register_grammar_backend(name, init_func)` (line 202-203)
- Server args determine which backend to use: `--grammar-backend` (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance, or none)

#### **Dispatch Mechanism:**
The constraint type determines which dispatch method is called:

```python
# From base_grammar_backend.py, _init_value_dispatch()
key_type, key_string = key
if key_type == "json":
    grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
elif key_type == "regex":
    grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
elif key_type == "ebnf":
    grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
elif key_type == "structural_tag":
    grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
    grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
    grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
else:
    grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
```

---

### 5. Constraint Flow from User Code to Backend

**Step 1: User Code (Public API)**
```python
sgl.gen(name="output", json_schema='{"type":"object"}')
sgl.gen(name="output", regex=r"\d+")
```

**Step 2: Language IR Layer**
- `gen()` function in `api.py` creates `SglGen` object with constraint parameters
- Stores in `SglGen.sampling_params` (of type `SglSamplingParams`)

**Step 3: Sampling Parameters**
- `SglSamplingParams` fields: `json_schema`, `regex`, `dtype`
- `to_srt_kwargs()` method passes constraints to SRT backend

**Step 4: Grammar Manager**
- `grammar_manager.process_req_with_grammar()` detects constraint and creates key:
  ```python
  if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
      key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
  elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
      key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
  elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
      key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
  elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
      key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
  ```

**Step 5: Backend Dispatch**
- Grammar backend's `get_cached_or_future_value()` retrieves or compiles grammar
- Dispatches to appropriate `dispatch_*()` method based on constraint type
- Returns `BaseGrammarObject` instance or cached result

**Step 6: Token Sampling**
- `SamplingBatchInfo` stores list of grammar objects: `grammars: List[BaseGrammarObject]`
- `update_regex_vocab_mask()` creates vocabulary masks by calling:
  - `grammar.allocate_vocab_mask()` - allocate mask tensor
  - `grammar.fill_vocab_mask()` - mark allowed tokens
  - `grammar.move_vocab_mask()` - move to device
- `apply_logits_bias()` calls `grammar.apply_vocab_mask()` to zero invalid logits

**Step 7: Token Generation**
- During decoding, accepted tokens are tracked:
  ```python
  if req.grammar is not None:
      req.grammar.accept_token(next_token_id)
  ```
- Grammar state is updated after each token

---

### 6. Existing Constraint Implementations

#### **XGrammar Backend:**
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
    else:
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
            schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
        )
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))

def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))

def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))

def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    # Handles legacy and new structural tag formats
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(...)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="structural_tag"))
```

#### **Outlines Backend:**
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
    regex = build_regex_from_object(key_string, whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern)
    return self._compile_regex(regex)

def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
    return self._compile_regex(key_string)
```

#### **LLGuidance Backend:**
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
    serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
        key_string,
        defaults={
            "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
            "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
        },
    )
    return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)

def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
    serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
    return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
```

---

### 7. Vocabulary Mask Application (In Sampling)

From `sampling_batch_info.py`:

```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    """Fill vocab masks for all grammars in batch"""
    if not self.grammars:
        return
    
    first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
    
    # Allocate once per batch
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
        vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
        batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
        device=self.device,
    )
    self.apply_mask_func = first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
    
    # Fill mask for each request's grammar
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    # Move to device
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)

def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    """Apply all biases including vocab mask"""
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

---

### 8. Key Features

1. **Mutual Exclusivity**: Only one constraint type per request (validated in `SamplingParams.verify()`)
2. **Caching**: Grammar compilation results are cached and reused across requests
3. **Async Compilation**: Grammar compilation happens asynchronously via ThreadPoolExecutor
4. **Multi-Backend Support**: XGrammar (primary), Outlines (fallback), LLGuidance
5. **Decorator Pattern**: ReasonerGrammarBackend wraps another backend for reasoning support
6. **Custom Backend Registry**: Extensible via `GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY`
7. **Reasoning Mode**: Special handling for models with thinking/reasoning phases
8. **Token-by-Token Validation**: Grammar state updated after each generated token
9. **Batch Processing**: All grammars in a batch updated in parallel

---

### 9. Test Files

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472700606-gwge3f/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py` - Tests JSON schema constraint generation

---

This comprehensive constraint system allows SGLang to enforce structured outputs at generation time by masking invalid tokens based on grammar rules.

### 3. Class Hierarchy for Constraints

```
BaseGrammarObject (abstract base)
├── XGrammarGrammar (xgrammar backend)
├── OutlinesGrammar (outlines backend)
├── GuidanceGrammar (llguidance backend)
└── ReasonerGrammarObject (wrapper for reasoning mode)

BaseGrammarBackend (abstract dispatcher)
├── XGrammarGrammarBackend
├── OutlinesGrammarBackend
├── GuidanceBackend
└── ReasonerGrammarBackend (wrapper)

GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY (global registry for custom backends)
```

### 4. Constraint Registration and Dispatch

#### **Registration:**
- Backends are created in `create_grammar_backend()` in `base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 206-274)
- Custom backends can be registered via `register_grammar_backend(name, init_func)` (line 202-203)
- Server args determine which backend to use: `--grammar-backend` (xgrammar, outlines, llguidance, or none)

#### **Dispatch Mechanism:**
The constraint type determines which dispatch method is called:

```python
# From base_grammar_backend.py, _init_value_dispatch()
key_type, key_string = key
if key_type == "json":
    grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
elif key_type == "regex":
    grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
elif key_type == "ebnf":
    grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
elif key_type == "structural_tag":
    grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
    grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
    grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
else:
    grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
```

### 5. Constraint Flow from User Code to Backend

**Step 1: User Code (Public API)**
```python
sgl.gen(name="output", json_schema='{"type":"object"}')
sgl.gen(name="output", regex=r"\d+")
```

**Step 2: Language IR Layer**
- `gen()` function in `api.py` creates `SglGen` object with constraint parameters
- Stores in `SglGen.sampling_params` (of type `SglSamplingParams`)

**Step 3: Sampling Parameters**
- `SglSamplingParams` fields: `json_schema`, `regex`, `dtype`
- `to_srt_kwargs()` method passes constraints to SRT backend

**Step 4: Grammar Manager**
- `grammar_manager.process_req_with_grammar()` detects constraint and creates key:
  ```python
  if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
      key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
  elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
      key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
  elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
      key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
  elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
      key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
  ```

**Step 5: Backend Dispatch**
- Grammar backend's `get_cached_or_future_value()` retrieves or compiles grammar
- Dispatches to appropriate `dispatch_*()` method based on constraint type
- Returns `BaseGrammarObject` instance or cached result

**Step 6: Token Sampling**
- `SamplingBatchInfo` stores list of grammar objects: `grammars: List[BaseGrammarObject]`
- `update_regex_vocab_mask()` creates vocabulary masks by calling:
  - `grammar.allocate_vocab_mask()` - allocate mask tensor
  - `grammar.fill_vocab_mask()` - mark allowed tokens
  - `grammar.move_vocab_mask()` - move to device
- `apply_logits_bias()` calls `grammar.apply_vocab_mask()` to zero invalid logits

**Step 7: Token Generation**
- During decoding, accepted tokens are tracked:
  ```python
  if req.grammar is not None:
      req.grammar.accept_token(next_token_id)
  ```
- Grammar state is updated after each token

### 6. Existing Constraint Implementations

#### **XGrammar Backend:**
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
    else:
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
            schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
        )
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))

def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))

def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))

def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    # Handles legacy and new structural tag formats
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(...)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="structural_tag"))
```

#### **Outlines Backend:**
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
    regex = build_regex_from_object(key_string, whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern)
    return self._compile_regex(regex)

def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
    return self._compile_regex(key_string)
```

#### **LLGuidance Backend:**
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
    serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
        key_string,
        defaults={
            "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
            "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
        },
    )
    return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)

def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
    serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
    return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
```

### 7. Vocabulary Mask Application (In Sampling)

From `sampling_batch_info.py`:

```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    """Fill vocab masks for all grammars in batch"""
    if not self.grammars:
        return
    
    first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
    
    # Allocate once per batch
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
        vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
        batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
        device=self.device,
    )
    self.apply_mask_func = first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
    
    # Fill mask for each request's grammar
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    # Move to device
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)

def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    """Apply all biases including vocab mask"""
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

---

### 8. Key Features

1. **Mutual Exclusivity**: Only one constraint type per request (validated in `SamplingParams.verify()`)
2. **Caching**: Grammar compilation results are cached and reused across requests
3. **Async Compilation**: Grammar compilation happens asynchronously via ThreadPoolExecutor
4. **Multi-Backend Support**: XGrammar (primary), Outlines (fallback), LLGuidance
5. **Decorator Pattern**: ReasonerGrammarBackend wraps another backend for reasoning support
6. **Custom Backend Registry**: Extensible via `GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY`
7. **Reasoning Mode**: Special handling for models with thinking/reasoning phases
8. **Token-by-Token Validation**: Grammar state updated after each generated token
9. **Batch Processing**: All grammars in a batch updated in parallel

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    add_to_grammar_queue = False
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
            key, req.require_reasoning
        )
        req.grammar = value
```

```python
def get_json_schema_constraint(
    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
) -> Optional[dict]:
    """Generate JSON schema constraint from tool definitions"""
    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
        for tool in tools:
            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
                return {
                    "type": "array",
                    "minItems": 1,
                    "maxItems": 1,
                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
                }
    elif tool_choice == "required":
        json_schema = {
            "type": "array",
            "minItems": 1,
            "items": {
                "type": "object",
                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
            },
        }
```

```
TokenizedGenerateReqInput (SamplingParams with constraints)
    ↓
Scheduler → GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()
    ↓
Grammar Backend (create constraint object)
    ↓
Req.grammar = grammar_object
    ↓
ScheduleBatch → SamplingBatchInfo
    ↓
update_regex_vocab_mask() - Create vocab_mask
    ↓
forward_pass() → apply_logits_bias() 
    ↓
apply_vocab_mask(logits, vocab_mask) - Restrict tokens
    ↓
sample_next_token() - Only sample from valid tokens
    ↓
Return constrained output
```

| Layer | File Path | Key Classes/Functions |
|-------|-----------|----------------------|
| Frontend API | `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | `gen()` function |
